summary.intent: Address a batch of Codex review findings on PR #724 in the ars-ui repo, covering reactive prop staleness in Dioxus Input, description registration ordering in Dioxus SSR, Leptos Form validation-behavior reactivity, and Leptos Fieldset controlled prop reactivity.
summary.outcome: All four review findings were fixed, wasm browser tests pass for both adapters, formatting and clippy are clean, and the changes were committed as a single batched commit. Coverage CI failure was identified as a Codecov GPG uploader issue unrelated to the code changes.

repo: machine.derive() only tracks machine signals (state/context), not arbitrary captured Rust values — plain Dioxus props captured inside it go stale.
Leptos Fieldset inherited context should expose the original Signal<bool> values directly rather than wrapping them in additional Memos to avoid reactive borrow panics during descendant field sync.

Dioxus VNode internals are public enough to inspect component names but not safe to walk for evaluating future child components from a parent, making pre-scan approaches for SSR description ordering unviable.

In Leptos, reactive attribute closures passed to custom_attribute are tracked by Leptos itself, so get() is correct there; get_untracked() is needed only in event handlers that are not reactive tracking contexts.
The clean adapter split is: machine.derive() for machine-driven attrs (aria-invalid, aria-describedby, data-ars-state), plain prop merging outside that memo for HTML attrs like type/name/placeholder.

code.finding: machine.derive() result is now called inline (no shadowed binding) and prop-dependent attrs are merged outside the memo so Dioxus component rerenders update them.

Inherited context now carries controlled Signal<bool> values directly; removed the intermediate Memo layer that caused reactive borrow panics when descendant fields derived inherited state during the same update wave.
Validation-behavior reactivity fixed; submit handler uses get_untracked() since event handlers are not reactive tracking contexts.
Leptos custom_attribute receives the reactive closure directly, so get() inside AttrValue::reactive_bool closures is correct — Leptos owns the tracking context when it materializes the attribute.

workflow: Batch all review findings from a Codex review pass before making any commit — avoids multiple CI roundtrips for closely related fixes.
For wasm-bindgen browser tests, the sandbox blocks wasm-bindgen-test-runner from spawning its browser test server without escalation.
Codecov uploader GPG key verification failures are infra-level flakes unrelated to code changes and can be distinguished from real coverage regressions by inspecting the job log.
cargo xtask ci fmt and cargo xclippy are the local gates that mirror CI fmt/clippy checks.
When clippy suggests converting a closure to a method item but the closure is required due to lifetime constraints, a targeted #[expect] on the specific statement is the correct suppression approach.

friction: wasm-bindgen-test-runner was blocked by the sandbox, requiring escalation to run browser tests.
The Leptos fieldset regression (reactive borrow panic) required two iterative patches before the root cause (Memo wrapping Signals in inherited context) was identified.
A typo in a GraphQL query for review threads caused one poll to fail silently and required a rerun.
Clippy suggested converting a closure to a method item but the method form hit a lifetime-generalization limitation, requiring a narrow #[expect] workaround.
The Dioxus SSR description ordering issue required reading Dioxus VNode internals to rule out a VNode-walker approach before settling on the deterministic ID merge fix.
GitHub transient connection errors interrupted one workflow run metadata fetch, requiring a retry.
open_items: Coverage CI failure due to Codecov GPG uploader key verification ('Can't check signature: No public key') is unresolved — needs a workflow-level fix or rerun independent of this PR.
The release verification CI job outcome was not confirmed before the commit; it was still pending when Codex review results arrived.

Dioxus props that must be observed across callback lifetimes (not just render-time) may still warrant Signal<T> — the distinction between render-time props and signal props in Dioxus adapters is not fully documented.
The block v0.1.6 future-incompat clippy note is a known standing issue not addressed in this batch.
